title: Playbook - Microsoft Graph Attack Simulation Retrieval
applies_to: Export attack simulation and simulation user results from Microsoft Graph
prerequisites: Tenant licensing: Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 2
Cloud environment must be Worldwide or GCC — Graph APIs for Attack Simulation are unavailable in GCC High and DoD
Admin consent for AttackSimulation.Read.All granted by Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator
Working app-only Graph token with the required security permissions
Simulation display name or simulation ID
CSV or downstream schema for flattened simulation user results
required_permissions: AttackSimulation.Read.All application permission for unattended access
request_sequence: List simulations: GET /v1.0/security/attackSimulation/simulations with optional OData filter (e.g. $filter=displayName eq '{name}')
If OData filter fails due to special characters in displayName, list all simulations (paginating with @odata.nextLink) and match client-side
Resolve the target simulation ID from displayName or other metadata

Get campaign overview: GET /v1.0/security/attackSimulation/simulations/{simulationId}/report/overview — returns resolvedTargetsCount, simulationEventsContent, trainingEventsContent, and recommendedActions; use as summary-level data for reporting
Follow @odata.nextLink until exhausted to collect all user records

Flatten each userSimulationDetails record: promote simulationUser fields (userId, displayName, email) to top level, expand simulationEvents[] by eventName, derive training summary from trainingEvents[].latestTrainingStatus

Write to CSV with columns: simulationName, userEmail, userDisplayName, isCompromised, compromisedDateTime, reportedPhishDateTime, assignedTrainingsCount, completedTrainingsCount, inProgressTrainingsCount, plus event/training columns as needed

confirmed_facts.fact: Campaign overview metrics are available from /v1.0/security/attackSimulation/simulations/{simulationId}/report/overview.
Simulation user results are returned from /v1.0/security/attackSimulation/simulations/{simulationId}/report/simulationUsers.
The simulationUsers endpoint supports $count, $skipToken, and $top, but not $filter, $select, or $orderby.
Pagination uses @odata.nextLink and skip tokens rather than numeric skip offsets.

userSimulationDetails CSV-relevant fields: isCompromised (Boolean), compromisedDateTime (DateTimeOffset), reportedPhishDateTime (DateTimeOffset), assignedTrainingsCount, completedTrainingsCount, inProgressTrainingsCount; simulationUser.email, simulationUser.displayName
Key simulationEvents eventName values observed in production: SuccessfullyDeliveredEmail, EmailLinkClicked, ReportedPhish
Attack sim Graph APIs are documented as unavailable in GCC High and DoD environments.

implementation_notes: Apply filtering on simulations endpoint (supports $filter on displayName), not on simulationUsers (no $filter support).
Have a client-side fallback when displayName filtering is brittle due to special characters (parentheses, slashes in name).
Normalize nested training and event arrays explicitly before CSV export.

Recommended CSV column set: simulationName, userEmail, userDisplayName, isCompromised, compromisedDateTime, reportedPhishDateTime, assignedTrainingsCount, completedTrainingsCount, inProgressTrainingsCount — expand simulationEvents and trainingEvents as additional columns as required by consumer.
Token caching: expires_in is ~3599s — cache and refresh before expiry to avoid per-request token overhead.
Use report/overview for summary-level tenant metrics (total targeted, click rates, training completion); use simulationUsers for per-user export and downstream training tracking.
common_failures: 403 when AttackSimulation.Read.All is missing or not admin-consented
404 when the simulation ID is wrong or stale
Partial exports if the client ignores nextLink pagination
OData $filter rejection for displayName values containing parentheses or slashes — fall back to client-side match
Missing simulationUser fields (null userId/email) for guest or deleted accounts — guard with None checks before writing CSV
403 or empty data when calling from GCC High or DoD tenant — Attack Simulation Graph APIs are not supported in those clouds even if the Defender portal product is available
Incomplete user data when on-premises mailboxes are in scope — expected reduced reporting behavior, not an auth error; document in prerequisites and troubleshooting
